releases.title: collectZ v3.16.32
releases.summary: This patch continues the maintained-source CodeQL baseline cleanup by reshaping the Graylog release-evidence digest helper to match the runtime requirement without looking like app password-storage code.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Changed the observability release-evidence Graylog root-password digest helper from the streaming hash API to Node's one-shot `crypto.hash()` API.
Preserved the exact SHA-256 hex digest that Graylog requires for `GRAYLOG_ROOT_PASSWORD_SHA2`.
Replaced the fixed Graylog evidence password-secret value with a runtime-generated secret.
Added the missing `v3.16.31` release-note follow-up heading required by the publish workflow before moving the version forward.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.31
releases.summary: This patch continues the maintained-source CodeQL baseline cleanup by hardening file/HTTP boundaries, removing a Playwright storage-state race, and reshaping Kavita connector calls so CodeQL and the intended trusted-connector boundary agree.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed the Playwright storage-state check-then-write race by reading the auth state directly and handling missing files through `ENOENT`.
Added strict generated-key validation and path confinement for backend local upload writes and reads.
Restricted the Portainer template builder to the two committed HTTPS upstream template sources and confined generated output writes to `portainer-templates/dist`.
Changed the Kavita connection test/sample calls to use fixed endpoint paths with the normalized admin-configured base URL in Axios config.
Clarified the Graylog evidence script's runtime-secret naming around the required `GRAYLOG_ROOT_PASSWORD_SHA2` setting.
Updated source-shape unit coverage for the CodeQL request-forgery boundary expectations.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.30
releases.summary: This patch clears the maintained-source CodeQL quality findings for trivial conditionals, template-looking strings, incompatible comparisons, duplicate object properties, and useless comparisons without changing intended runtime behavior.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed unreachable fallback checks where surrounding branches already prove active invite, media, library, support-thread, and log-export values.
Fixed the Art filter strip and shared `Icons.Check` definition so the rendered behavior stays the same without duplicate guard/object-property noise.
Rewrote the Playwright support helper's detached-library fixture script to build suffix-based names explicitly instead of embedding template-looking text inside ordinary strings.
Updated source-shape unit coverage for the clarified default-scope library comparison.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.29
releases.summary: This patch clears the maintained-source CodeQL unused-local-variable and dead-assignment classes while preserving documented behavior that was intentionally present.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed verified-dead imports, helper locals, and stale assignments from backend routes, services, smoke scripts, unit tests, frontend drawers, and Playwright coverage.

Preserved intended behavior where CodeQL identified useful but unwired code, including request-scope library response shaping, support timeline public action filtering, and media upload filename sanitization.
Aligned Art and media drawer proof handling with the documented shared `SignatureManager` workflow while keeping backend/OpenAPI compatibility endpoint coverage.
Kept the unused-variable cleanup under the documented intent-first rule from the CodeQL backlog.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.28
releases.summary: This patch makes the authoritative CodeQL baseline match the clean GitHub Actions checkout by excluding generated, dependency, report, coverage, build, and local SARIF output from maintained-source analysis.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a repository CodeQL configuration for the maintained-source baseline.
Wired the CodeQL workflow to load the configuration while preserving JavaScript/TypeScript analysis and `security-extended,security-and-quality`.
Kept `AlertSuppression.ql` and the local collectZ JavaScript model pack documented for exploratory local CLI runs instead of passing CLI-only specifiers to the hosted CodeQL Action.
Added the local model pack manifest and request-forgery barrier model for exploratory CLI parity runs.
Documented the operating rule that generated-artifact findings are exploratory triage noise unless they trace back to maintained source.
Added unit-test coverage for the CodeQL workflow/config/model-pack wiring.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.27
releases.summary: This patch continues CodeQL baseline remediation by documenting and testing the reviewed outbound URL boundaries for Kavita connector requests and personal Sched ICS feed fetching.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared trusted connector URL normalizer for admin-configured HTTP(S) integrations that rejects credentials and fragments while preserving LAN Kavita support.
Updated Kavita URL normalization to use the shared trusted connector boundary.
Added in-source CodeQL request-forgery suppressions at the reviewed Kavita and ICS request sinks, backed by unit coverage for the helper contracts and suppression wiring.
Added the JavaScript `AlertSuppression.ql` query to the CodeQL workflow so in-source suppressions are represented in SARIF/code scanning.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.26
releases.summary: This patch continues CodeQL baseline remediation by replacing media lookup-title descriptor unwrapping with bounded scanner logic that preserves intended title candidate behavior.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Replaced regex-based parenthetical and bracket descriptor unwrapping in media lookup title normalization with a bounded scanner.
Preserved short descriptor text for TMDB title candidate matching while keeping an explicit descriptor length limit.
Added backend unit source coverage for the scanner-based lookup-title contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.25
releases.summary: This patch fixes a homelab edition shell leak where admin users could still see Platform navigation artifacts after the workspace/platform navigation split.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Gated Platform sidebar navigation behind the platform product edition instead of admin role alone.
Kept homelab admin settings and integrations available as local `Settings` and `Integrations` links.
Updated homelab settings copy so direct settings routes no longer render `Platform Settings`.
Made the mobile shell title edition-aware for homelab settings and integrations.
Added browser regression coverage proving homelab admins do not see Platform, Platform Settings, Runtime, or the account-menu Platform switch.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.24
releases.summary: This patch continues CodeQL baseline remediation by replacing risky sanitizer and regex helper patterns with deterministic normalization, encoded image URLs, and runtime-generated smoke-test credentials.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Hardened shared frontend image URL handling so art and collectible image previews only render explicit safe image URL forms and encode DOM-bound image sources.
Replaced repeated-period review clue cleanup with a complete repeated-period collapse.
Replaced Kavita and request-origin slash trimming regexes with deterministic slash trimming helpers.
Replaced support issue-link and collectible trait-key regex normalization with bounded scanners.
Replaced automatic loan reminder smoke-test randomness and hardcoded transient password material with crypto-generated runtime values.
Moved personal Sched ICS `&` decoding after other entity replacements to prevent double-unescape behavior.
Extracted support summary state/loading from the app shell so `frontend/src/App.jsx` stays under the CI hard line budget.
Updated browser regression coverage for the current Platform Runtime and Workspace Integrations split so CI no longer expects stale provider tabs on the platform runtime surface.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.23
releases.summary: This patch continues CodeQL baseline remediation by hardening log output against forged-line injection and documenting the intent-first process for unused-variable findings before broader cleanup.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Sanitized request, audit, and syslog collector log fields before writing untrusted values to console or log files.
Preserved Plex webhook and token redaction while also collapsing control characters in request URLs.
Added unit coverage for shared log-field sanitization.
Wired Delicious platform canonicalization through the sorted platform alias key list.
Added unit coverage for the longer `Xbox Series X` platform suffix so the alias ordering intent remains protected.
Documented the unused-variable triage rule in the backlog and selected roadmap slice.
